title: Chest pain safety-net documentation

red_flags.text: Lists life-threatening chest-pain patterns, associated breathlessness, syncope, diaphoresis, abnormal physiology, and emergency referral requirements.
red_flags.rationale: The DHA section supports documenting assessed high-risk symptoms, abnormal observations, and clinician escalation concern as prompts; it does not generate an emergency conclusion or instruction.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists life-threatening chest-pain patterns, associated breathlessness, syncope, diaphoresis, abnormal physiology, and emergency referral requirements.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Chest Pain – 45

investigations.text: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.
investigations.rationale: The NICE section supports recording concerns, uncertainty, reviewed-investigation explanation, questions, and patient-specific recurrent-pain advice only when actually discussed by a clinician.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.

source.title: Recent-onset chest pain of suspected cardiac origin: assessment and diagnosis

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1.1-1.1.1.9 - information, uncertainty, test discussion, and recurrent-pain advice

source_limitations: Actual symptoms, acuity, vital signs, assessment, cause, tests, disposition, specific warning signs, emergency route, contact details, comprehension, written instructions, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
